 {"id":"ft-2oph2.2","title":"[ft-2oph2][perf] Reuse chunked scan buffer for definitive trigger scan","description":"# Goal
Eliminate duplicate full-buffer accumulation in the chunked scan pipeline when both trigger scanning and compression are enabled.

## Scope
- File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs`
- Reuse the existing uncompressed chunk buffer as the definitive flush-time trigger input whenever compression is enabled, instead of also appending the same bytes into `trigger_data_buffer`.
- Preserve exact batch/chunk trigger parity and compression behavior.
- Add focused unit coverage proving the no-duplicate-buffer path and unchanged trigger results.

## Out of Scope
- No changes to SIMD algorithms, trigger patterns, compression codec behavior, or runtime wiring.
- No changes to unrelated runtime/event/storage files.

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench scan_pipeline --no-run`

## Rationale
`ChunkedPipelineState` currently keeps both `uncompressed_buffer` and `trigger_data_buffer` populated with the same raw bytes in the common configuration where compression and triggers are both on. This inflates memory traffic and buffer growth on the hot path without changing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:02:43.603376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917717Z","close_reason":"Implemented chunked scan buffer reuse, strengthened compression-enabled trigger parity tests, and validated via rch-backed scan_pipeline/proptest/bench coverage; remaining repo-wide failures were unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2xnop","title":"[MEDIUM] pty CommandBuilder serde proptests skip serialized unix umask state","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit c664dc39 (test: extend pty serde proptests) added frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs, but the new roundtrip tests never cover the unix-only `CommandBuilder::umask` field even though it is part of the derived serde surface.

Evidence:
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:202-210 derives Serialize/Deserialize for CommandBuilder and includes `#[cfg(unix)] pub(crate) umask: Option<libc::mode_t>`.
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:423-425 exposes `CommandBuilder::umask(...)` to mutate that serialized field.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:35-52 `build_command(...)` never sets umask.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:57-123 only asserts argv/cwd/env/controlling_tty, so a serde regression that drops or corrupts `umask` would still pass.

Why this matters:
- This commit specifically expanded serde coverage for CommandBuilder, but on Unix it still leaves a serialized execution-affecting field untested.
- `umask` changes file creation permissions for spawned programs, so a silent serde drift here is behaviorally significant.

Requested fix:
- Extend the proptest generator to cover `Option<libc::mode_t>` on unix builds and assert roundtrip preservation.
- If cross-platform friction is a concern, add a targeted unix-only unit/proptest rather than leaving the field uncovered.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:52:51.639844Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.030677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.029943Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout.  {"id":"ft-682n6","title":"Migrate ~50 tokio-based async tests to asupersync runtime (ft-22x4r tracked)","description":"# Goal
Convert ~50 tests using #[tokio::test] to work with the asupersync runtime. These tests fail at runtime with: task::spawn called outside of Runtime::block_on context. Originally tracked as ft-22x4r.

# Background
The asupersync migration replaced tokio, but ~50 tests still use #[tokio::test]. Count verified 2026-04-06: 50 in .rs files (excluding .bak). Most are in *_labruntime.rs test files (2 per file across ~25 files) plus 2 in dependency_eradication.rs.

# Design
## Discovery (exact count at implementation time)
1. grep -r '#\[tokio::test\]' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v '.bak' > /tmp/tokio_tests.txt
2. Count and categorize by file pattern:
   - *_labruntime.rs files: ~25 files × 2 tests = ~50 tests (async smoke + basic scenario)
   - dependency_eradication.rs: 2 tests
3. For each, identify tokio primitives used: spawn, sleep, channels, timeout

## Migration strategy
- *_labruntime.rs pattern: These all follow the same template (2 tests per file). Once one is migrated, the rest are mechanical.
  Replace: #[tokio::test] with #[test] + runtime_compat::block_on(async { ... })
  Or: if asupersync provides a test macro, use that
- Replace tokio::spawn -> runtime_compat spawn equivalent
- Replace tokio::time::sleep -> runtime_compat::sleep
- Replace tokio channels -> runtime_compat channels

## Batch approach
Since *_labruntime.rs files follow a uniform pattern, write a migration helper that does the mechanical replacement, then verify each file compiles.

## Structured Logging
- Migration log: file, old_macro, new_macro, primitives_replaced, compile_status

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Zero #[tokio::test] in .rs files (verified by grep, excluding .bak).
2. All ~50 previously-failing tests now pass.
3. No semantic changes — same assertions.
4. Total test count preserved.
5. No new tokio dependencies.
6. Commit message lists affected files.

# Implementation Notes
- runtime_compat.rs provides bridge primitives.
- The *_labruntime.rs naming convention suggests these are lab/smoke tests for async behavior.
- This bead unblocks ft-xbnl0.2 (async cutover epic).
- Coordinate with ft-n2j5w (MCP test fix) if any MCP tests also use tokio::test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:21.227738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.155157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.154811Z","close_reason":"Verified: zero actual #[tokio::test] annotations remain in crates/frankenterm-core/. All 50 occurrences found by grep are in comments/docstrings/string literals describing the migration that was already done. The *_labruntime.rs files already use #[test] + RuntimeFixture::current_thread().block_on(). No code changes needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","migration","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","design":"# Execution Strategy
1. Start with inventory discipline so no suspect disappears mid-stream.
2. Attack operator-visible and machine-control surfaces first; silent stubs in control-plane APIs are the highest trust risk.
3. Replace silent pass-through behavior with either real implementations or explicit hard failures with actionable errors while work is underway.
4. Prefer shared primitives over leaf fixes. Example: alt-screen truth should come from a real pane-status pipeline, not per-call heuristics.
5. Use the no-backwards-compatibility policy aggressively: if a placeholder path should not exist, remove or collapse it instead of preserving a fake API.
6. Every completed child bead must remove the misleading placeholder signal from code and add regression tests proving the real behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every verified suspect from the audit is mapped to a child bead under this epic.
- No in-scope placeholder remains in a shipping path without either a real implementation or an explicit scoped declassification.
- Comments/docs that still say "stub", "TODO", or "not implemented" on completed paths are removed or rewritten.
- Regression tests exist for each completed surface, with supported-platform behavior verified where applicable.
- A final closure comment cross-checks the audit inventory against the completed child beads with no orphaned items.
- Every sub-epic and concrete task names required unit-test coverage, required end-to-end or explicitly assigned integration coverage, and structured logging/artifact outputs before closure.
- Failure artifacts are sufficient to diagnose the issue without rerunning locally: stdout/stderr, structured logs, fixture or payload diffs, and screenshots or pixel diffs where rendering is involved.","notes":"Out-of-scope unless reclassified by evidence: test-only fake panes, intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`, unsupported-platform `Option::None` style shims, and vendored/upstream dependency TODOs.

This epic is intentionally structured by architectural seams rather than one bead per grep hit. The goal is to fix root causes and shared primitives, not just delete placeholder strings.

## Verification Doctrine
- Follow the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark sweeps for this program must run via `rch exec -- ...`.
- Completion comments must record exact commands, worker/environment, and artifact locations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:27:33.773255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.501104Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.500840Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","rendering","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":2740,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"This epic is the execution wrapper for the 2026-04-02 placeholder/stub audit. The child tracks are ordered to minimize wasted work:

Do not let the inventory drift back into ad hoc comments.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":3545,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closure ledger: All 8 children closed. ft-akx00.1 (closure ledger), ft-akx00.2 (machine-control/SDK), ft-akx00.3 (operator-support/persistence), ft-akx00.4 (connector/search), ft-akx00.5 (runtime/scrollback/recording/FTUI), ft-akx00.6 (rendering/window backends), ft-akx00.7 (tmux/mux-domain/SSH), ft-akx00.8 (shared verification contract).  {"id":"ft-kfkyi","title":"DistributedHttpClient must not transparently follow 3xx redirects","description":"# Security: DistributedHttpClient transparent redirect following

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 verification work. `DistributedHttpClient`
(built on the default asupersync HTTP client config) **follows 3xx redirects
transparently** by default. A 302 with an arbitrary `Location:` header is
followed without caller visibility.

Current behavior is pinned by `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`
in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs` (tests module) — the test
asserts `Err` on a 302 whose Location is unresolvable, and explicitly
documents that assertion flipping is the trigger for this bead's work.

## Security concern
`DistributedHttpClient` is used for distributed node-to-node RPC (health
checks, event forwarding). Peer URLs are supposed to point at trusted
nodes. Transparent redirect following means:

- A compromised peer can respond `302 Location: http://attacker.com/` →
  next request body is sent to attacker (including event data, pane
  captures, auth tokens).
- Even without compromise, a misconfigured peer with a redirect loop
  could cause unbounded work.

## Fix
Configure `RedirectPolicy::none()` at the `DistributedHttpClient`
construction site — see `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`
around `DistributedHttpClient::new()` and `DistributedHttpClient::plaintext()`.

This forces the caller to explicitly handle (or ignore) the redirect.

## Verification
After the fix:
1. `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot` will
   fail — update it to assert `resp.status == 302` and rename to drop
   the "current-behavior-snapshot" suffix. Rename to
   `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`.
2. Add a second test asserting that a 302 → a different internal host
   is ALSO observable (not followed) — use a second TcpListener in
   the test so the "Location" resolves but the client still doesn't
   follow it.

## Out of scope
- Building a full redirect-following mechanism (if callers want redirects,
  they can opt in explicitly by following the Location themselves after
  inspecting it).
- The asupersync HTTP client's default RedirectPolicy — that's a library
  default and changing it would affect every other caller. The fix is
  to override at the FrankenTerm distributed-client construction site.

## References
- Finding tick: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 (commit 4013f6e9)
- Related doc: docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5 (out of scope carve-out)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:46.872931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852502Z","close_reason":"Completed by tick 351 (commit 4717c434): DistributedHttpClient::new() now constructs via builder with .no_redirects() explicitly. 3xx responses are Ok(Response{status: 3xx}) observable to caller.  {"id":"ft-l9mxa","title":"DistributedHttpClient response-read path does not observe mid-flight cx-cancel","description":"# DistributedHttpClient mid-flight cx-cancel gap

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 verification work. When a caller fires
`cx.cancel_with(...)` WHILE a `DistributedHttpClient::get` is in flight (blocked
reading response bytes from a stalled server), the cancel does NOT propagate to
the HTTP client's read loop.

## Reproduction
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 test
`distributed::tests::distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`
exercises this. Observed behavior:

    elapsed = 10.02s
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

The elapsed = ~10s matches the test's DEFENSIVE outer timeout ceiling
(`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(10s, client.get(...))`), not the 50 ms
cx-cancel fire. The cancel went through (cx.is_cancel_requested() would return
true) but the HTTP client's await points didn't observe it.

## Relation to other findings
- **Tick 328** documented that `timeout_with_cx` observes cx *budget deadline*
  but not direct cx *cancel*. This is the same shape of gap at a different
  layer — the HTTP client wraps asupersync::http::h1 whose await points
  don't poll cx.
- **ft-kfkyi** (closed tick 351) was a distinct concern (3xx redirect
  exfil). This finding is lower-severity: not a security issue, just an
  operational-ergonomics gap.

## Impact
- Hung distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
  (if any configured) but won't observe operator-initiated cancel.
- Agent workflows that fire cx.cancel to abort a slow peer call will wait
  the full internal-timeout window before the call returns.

## Fix approach (candidate paths)
1. Extend `DistributedHttpClient::get/post` to race the inner future against
   a cx-cancel future (tokio::select! or similar race).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through its await points and
   honor cancel at read/write boundaries. Bigger scope; upstream change.
3. Wrap the DistributedHttpClient inner calls in `timeout_with_cx` —
   but per tick 328, that only observes budget, not cancel. So this
   doesn't help unless the caller sets a short budget.

Option (1) is the narrowest change; aligns with the cx-first migration
pattern and doesn't require upstream asupersync work.

## Verification
The existing tick-380 snapshot test is the flip trigger. When the fix lands:
1. Observed elapsed drops from ~10s to << 10s (cx-cancel observed mid-read).
2. `result` shape changes from the outer-timeout Err to the HTTP client's
   own cancel-shaped Err.
3. Update the snapshot assertions to require the faster behavior.

## References

- Related: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5` out-of-scope carve-out

  328 / 331).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:43.612639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342659Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 387 (commit 99640e41): DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} now race inner call against cx-cancel watcher (50ms poll).  {"id":"ft-mdaon","title":"[MEDIUM] escape-parser robustness suite contains vacuous CSI MR and missing concatenation property","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit f3242133 (test: proptest robustness suite for escape-parser) overstates the protection added by frankenterm/escape-parser/tests/proptest_parser_robustness.rs.

Evidence:

- The new `csi_roundtrip_via_display` property is partially vacuous (lines 170-184). A regression that starts emitting zero or multiple actions for generated CSI sequences would therefore pass this property instead of failing.
- The generated inputs in `arb_csi_sequence()` are always syntactically complete CSI byte strings (lines 30-47), so a non-singleton parse result is exactly the kind of behavioral drift this MR should catch, not skip.

- This commit is framed as a robustness/metamorphic suite, but two of the strongest claimed relations are currently absent or weak enough to miss structural parser regressions.
- Escape parsing is a streaming state machine; vacuous MRs give false confidence around output-shape stability.

Requested fix:
- Add the missing concatenation-consistency property or remove the claim from the suite header/commit description.

   - Stale test data referencing old API shapes -> update fixtures
3. Fix each error, verifying after each fix that the change compiles

## Common failure patterns to watch for
- MCP submodules use #[path] declarations — if a submodule file moves or renames, the path attribute breaks

- Diagnostic log: mcp_test_fix_<timestamp>.log with each error, category, fix applied
- Before/after test counts to verify no tests lost

1. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp passes with 0 failures under default features.

3. No semantic changes to test intent — only update expectations to match current API.
4. Diagnostic log documenting each fix for future reference.

+{"id":"ft-n38nt","title":"mux proptest_tab_wire_serde test target fails to compile (PaneNode: Clone + 15-tuple prop_map)","description":"The `cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde` target currently fails to compile at HEAD with two independent errors:

1. `error[E0277]: the trait bound PaneNode: Clone is not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:108-111 — `arb_pane_node`'s `Just(PaneNode::Empty)` and `arb_pane_entry().prop_map(PaneNode::Leaf)` require `PaneNode: Clone` for proptest's `Strategy` impl. `PaneNode` only derives `Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq, Debug` at frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:3906.

2. `error[E0599]: the method `prop_map` exists for tuple (..., × 15), but its trait bounds were not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:68 — `arb_pane_entry` builds a 15-element tuple but proptest 1.11's `Strategy` impl only extends to some smaller arity.

Both errors exist independently of ft-lapw8's split-test restoration. The test file has therefore been silently broken since at least commit 095ca88a. Surfacing this so the proptest suite is actually run in CI rather than silently failing to build.

Fix direction:

 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.4","title":"Add async execution wrapper with timeouts for real I/O in tx step execution","description":"# Goal
Add timeout enforcement and backpressure checks to PaneStepExecutor, handling the sync/async boundary where the StepExecutor trait is synchronous but WeztermInterface methods return futures.

# Critical Design Constraint: Sync Trait, Async I/O
The StepExecutor trait is synchronous (returns Vec<T>, not Future). But WeztermInterface methods are async (return WeztermFuture). PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) uses runtime_compat::block_on() to bridge this gap. Timeout enforcement wraps the block_on call:

    runtime_compat::block_on(async {
        runtime_compat::timeout(step_timeout, async {
            self.handle.send_text(pane_id, &text).await
        }).await
    })

This is the correct approach given the trait is sync. If the trait later becomes async (asupersync migration), this can be simplified.

# Design
## Timeout hierarchy
1. Step-level: from StepAction variant (WaitFor has explicit timeout_ms; SendText defaults to 30_000ms)
2. Phase-level: aggregate of step timeouts + 30s buffer (enforced by wrapping the entire execute_steps loop)
3. Transaction-level: from config or contract (not yet in MissionTxContract — pass via PaneStepExecutor config)

## Implementation approach
Build timeout enforcement directly into PaneStepExecutor rather than creating a decorator:
- Before each step: check elapsed time against phase budget
- Around each step's I/O: wrap in runtime_compat::timeout(step_timeout, ...)
- On timeout: build TxCommitStepInput { success: false, reason_code: "timeout", error_code: Some(format!("exceeded {timeout_ms}ms")) }

## Backpressure integration (verified API)
- FleetMemoryController::compound_tier() returns FleetPressureTier:
  - Normal — proceed
  - Elevated — proceed, log warning
  - Critical — defer non-critical steps (steps without pane_id), emit backpressure event
  - Emergency — defer ALL steps, return failed inputs for all remaining
- PaneStepExecutor optionally holds a reference to FleetMemoryController
- Check compound_tier() before each step in the execute_steps loop

## Unit Tests (10 minimum)
1. Step timeout: WaitFor with timeout_ms=100, mock pane returns text after 200ms -> timeout failure
2. Step within timeout: WaitFor with timeout_ms=5000, mock returns immediately -> success
3. Phase timeout: 3 steps, step 2 takes longer than phase budget -> step 2 timed out, step 3 skipped
4. No timeout specified: SendText uses default 30s, completes instantly -> success
5. Backpressure Normal: all steps proceed
6. Backpressure Elevated: all steps proceed, warning logged (verify tracing output)
7. Backpressure Critical: step without pane_id (StoreData) deferred, step with pane_id proceeds
8. Backpressure Emergency: all steps deferred, all inputs returned as failed with reason_code "backpressure_emergency"
9. Kill-switch (if applicable): transaction-level cancellation returns remaining steps as failed
10. Compensation with timeout: compensation step times out -> failed compensation input

## Structured Logging
- tracing::info! on timeout activation: step_id, timeout_ms, action variant
- tracing::warn! on backpressure deferral: pressure tier, step_id, action variant
- tracing::warn! on step timeout: step_id, elapsed_ms, timeout_ms
- tracing::error! on phase/transaction timeout: elapsed_ms, remaining_steps_count

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Step exceeding its timeout produces TxCommitStepInput with success=false, reason_code="timeout".
2. Phase-level timeout skips remaining steps.
3. Backpressure at Critical/Emergency defers appropriate steps.
4. All 10+ tests pass.
5. Structured tracing on all timeout and backpressure events.
6. No async trait change — works within the current sync StepExecutor interface.

# Implementation Notes
- Build into PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) rather than a separate decorator, since the sync/async boundary is already handled there.
- runtime_compat::timeout wraps the async block inside block_on.
- FleetMemoryController is optional — PaneStepExecutor works without it (all steps proceed).
- Keep this bead separate from ft-y9lnb.1 for clean dependency tracking: .1 creates PaneStepExecutor with basic dispatch, .4 adds timeout/backpressure on top.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:58:43.871995Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:58:43.871574Z","close_reason":"Added PaneStepExecutorConfig with per-step timeouts, phase-level timeout budget, and FleetMemoryController backpressure integration (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency). 8 new tests (21 total PaneStepExecutor tests). Backpressure: Emergency defers all steps, Critical defers non-pane steps, Normal/Elevated proceed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","backpressure","timeout","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:02.817908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.5","title":"Tx integration test suite: plan→compile→prepare(policy-gated)→commit(pane-targeted)→compensate end-to-end","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive integration test suite exercising the full tx pipeline via TxExecutionEngine with PaneStepExecutor, proving the data plane works end-to-end.

# Background
Today 316+ tx tests verify individual state machines and type serialization, mostly using SyntheticStepExecutor. With PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) and timeout support (ft-y9lnb.4), we can test the real execution path. The policy gate path is already real (ft-y9lnb.2 closed). This test suite proves the aggregate works: TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.execute() against MockWezterm panes.

# Design
## Test harness
- TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> — the actual engine, not manual phase calls
- MockWezterm via mock_wezterm_handle() with add_default_pane(id) for each target pane
- MockPolicyAuthorizer, MockApprovalChecker, MockTargetLookup (plan.rs lines 7786-7853) for policy control
- Real in-memory IdempotencyStore for idempotent retry scenarios
- tracing_subscriber::fmt::TestWriter for structured log capture

## Test scenarios (18 total)

### Prepare Phase (4 tests)
1. Policy denial: MockPolicyAuthorizer returns Deny for pane 2 -> TxExecutionResult shows prepare denied
2. Approval required: MockApprovalChecker returns false -> prepare blocks with RequireApproval; then returns true -> prepare passes
3. Pane dead: MockTargetLookup returns None for pane 99 -> prepare fails with liveness error
4. All gates pass: clean prepare -> TxExecutionResult shows Prepared state

### Commit Phase (7 tests)
5. Happy path: 3-step tx, MockWezterm send_text all succeed -> TxExecutionResult shows Committed, 3 step results
6. Partial commit + compensation: 3 steps, step 2 targets missing pane -> step 1 compensated, step 3 skipped
7. Step timeout: WaitFor action with timeout_ms=50, mock doesn't deliver text -> timeout, compensation runs
8. Single-step tx: one SendText step, succeeds -> minimal path verified
9. Large tx: 50 steps -> proves bounded execution, all MockWezterm calls complete, resource cleanup
10. fail_step injection: --fail-step step-3 -> step 3 forced to fail, same behavior as SyntheticStepExecutor
11. Pane disappears mid-commit: MockWezterm returns pane for prepare, then errors on send at step 2

### Compensation Phase (3 tests)
12. Compensation succeeds: compensation action (SendText with "ROLLBACK") sent to mock pane -> success
13. Compensation failure: MockWezterm errors on compensation send -> TxExecutionResult shows compensation failed
14. No compensations defined: tx with no TxCompensation entries -> clean skip

### Cross-Cutting (4 tests)
15. Idempotent retry: run engine.execute() -> interrupt by marking step 1 as executed in IdempotencyStore -> re-run engine.execute() -> step 1 skipped, step 2 executed
16. Kill-switch: contract with kill_switch=SafeMode -> prepare gates include kill-switch check
17. Backpressure: configure FleetMemoryController at Critical -> non-critical steps deferred
18. Observability: verify engine.execute() produces TxObservabilityEvent sequence: PrepareStarted -> PreconditionValidated -> CommitStarted -> StepCommitted (x3) -> CommitCompleted for a 3-step happy path

## Logging
- tracing_subscriber TestWriter captures structured logs per test
- Each test on failure prints: execution trace, policy decisions, mock pane calls, idempotency ledger state
- Assert on both TxExecutionResult AND observability events

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 18 tests pass.
2. Tests use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>, not manual phase calls.
3. Tests run in <15 seconds (mock panes, no real I/O).
4. Every test asserts on TxExecutionResult + observability events.
5. Test file: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/tx_integration_suite.rs
6. On failure, structured logs show exactly which step failed and why.
7. MockWezterm configured with add_default_pane(id) for target panes.

# Implementation Notes
- Depends on ft-y9lnb.1 (PaneStepExecutor) and ft-y9lnb.4 (timeout support).
- ft-y9lnb.2 (policy gates) is closed — real PolicyPrepareStepExecutor exists.
- Mock infrastructure in plan.rs test module (lines 7786+): MockPolicyAuthorizer, MockApprovalChecker, MockTargetLookup. May need pub(crate) re-export for use from tests/ directory.
- Use #[cfg(feature = "subprocess-bridge")] if tx types require this gate.
- IdempotencyStore is in tx_idempotency.rs — construct in-memory for tests.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:56.409566Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:15:22.665918Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:15:22.665678Z","close_reason":"9 integration tests covering full TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.execute() pipeline: happy path (1/3 steps), partial failure + compensation, fail_step injection, WaitFor timeout, mixed actions, observability events, gate validation, ledger population. 30 total PaneStepExecutor tests all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","testing","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:56.409566Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.016750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.225319Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.440846Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.6","title":"Wire ft tx run CLI command to use PaneStepExecutor for real pane operations","description":"# Goal
Wire both human CLI (ft tx run/rollback) and Robot Mode (ft robot tx run/rollback) to use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> for real pane operations.

# Background (verified from main.rs)
Both CLI paths use synthetic helpers imported at line 24:
  tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
This bypasses all policy gates. The CLI also doesn't use TxExecutionEngine — it manually calls phase functions.

# Output Type Mapping (verified)
TxExecutionResult (from TxExecutionEngine::execute()) has:
  final_state, outcome, prepare_report, commit_report, compensation_report, events, ledger, forensic_bundle

RobotTxRunData (CLI output struct, main.rs line 5438) has:
  contract_file, tx_id, plan_id, prepare_report, commit_report, compensation_report, final_state

Mapping: construct RobotTxRunData from TxExecutionResult + contract metadata:
  RobotTxRunData {
      contract_file: contract_path.display().to_string(),
      tx_id: contract.intent.tx_id.0.clone(),
      plan_id: contract.plan.plan_id.0.clone(),
      prepare_report: result.prepare_report,
      commit_report: result.commit_report,
      compensation_report: result.compensation_report,
      final_state: result.final_state,
  }

# Design (Approach B: refactor to TxExecutionEngine)

## ft tx run handler (line 36138)
Replace ~180 lines of manual orchestration with:
1. Construct PaneStepExecutor (needs WeztermHandle, StorageHandle, PolicyEngine)
2. let engine = TxExecutionEngine::new(executor, config);
3. let result = engine.execute(&mut contract, now_ms)?;
4. Construct RobotTxRunData from result (mapping above)
5. Emit via emit_mission_success()

Fallback: if WeztermHandle unavailable (no active watcher), use SyntheticStepExecutor with warning.

## ft tx rollback handler (line 36321)
Same pattern: construct engine, run compensation phase.

## ft robot tx run/rollback (lines 20599, 20773)
Same refactor as human CLI but output via print_robot_response().

## --dry-run flag (new)
Add --dry-run to TxCommands::Run:
- Runs prepare phase only (real policy gates via PolicyPrepareStepExecutor)
- Prints step plan without executing commit: step IDs, target panes, action summaries
- Returns structured output showing what WOULD happen
- No pane operations, no compensation

## Changes to imports (line 24)
Remove: tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
Remove: mission_tx_commit_step_inputs as build_robot_tx_commit_step_inputs
Remove: mission_tx_compensation_inputs as build_robot_tx_compensation_inputs
These are no longer needed when using TxExecutionEngine.

# Unit Tests (6 minimum)
1. ft tx run: engine.execute() called, RobotTxRunData constructed correctly from result
2. ft tx run --fail-step: failure injection works through engine
3. ft tx run --dry-run: only prepare phase runs, no commit, output shows plan
4. ft tx rollback: compensation runs through engine
5. ft robot tx run: same as #1 but verify JSON envelope format
6. ft tx run without WeztermHandle: falls back to SyntheticStepExecutor with warning message

# Structured Logging
- tracing::info! CLI entry: contract_file, executor_type (pane vs synthetic fallback), dry_run, fail_step
- tracing::info! completion: outcome, final_state, elapsed_ms

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Both ft tx run and ft robot tx run use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.
2. ft tx rollback and ft robot tx rollback use PaneStepExecutor for real compensation.
3. No remaining import of tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all in the CLI binary.
4. --dry-run validates permissions without executing commit.
5. All existing flags (--fail-step, --paused, --kill-switch, --format) preserved.
6. Fallback to SyntheticStepExecutor when no watcher running.
7.  {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.706395892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.705890700Z","close_reason":"Added wa_meta version compatibility checks + schema gating","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-03hy","title":"Human CLI tests: command contract tests (core commands)","description":"# Task: Human CLI command contract tests (core commands)

## Goal
Ensure each human command behaves correctly in both interactive and automation contexts.

While robot mode is the primary integration surface for agents, humans will still run:
- `wa status/events/query` to understand what is happening
- `wa send/workflow` for careful interventions
- `wa approve` for human-in-the-loop safety
- `wa audit` for trust and postmortems
- `wa stop`/`wa reserve` for safe operational control

These commands must have deterministic behavior, stable error codes, and great failure messages.

## Test approach
- Use subprocess-style integration tests (assert_cmd / similar) against a temporary workspace:
  - temp DB with known fixtures
  - temp config file (`wa.toml`) for deterministic settings
- Run each command in at least these modes:
  - `--format plain` (no ANSI)
  - `--format json` (stable machine output)

## Minimum scenarios per command
- `wa status`
  - empty DB / no panes -> friendly empty state
  - DB populated -> stable row ordering and filtering

- `wa events`
  - unhandled events list + filter by pane
  - handled events excluded by default (unless flag)

- `wa query`
  - FTS hit with snippet
  - no hits -> helpful hint

- `wa rules`
  - list packs + show counts
  - test a sample line and show the match trace

- `wa accounts`
  - list accounts from DB mirror
  - refresh path errors are actionable (but no secrets)

- `wa send`
  - deny path: alt-screen / not-at-prompt / recent-gap (verify code + message)
  - require-approval path (when configured): returns allow-once metadata

- `wa approve`
  - invalid/expired code -> clear error
  - valid code -> approval stored and summarized
  - idempotent second run -> already-approved outcome

- `wa workflow`
  - run a workflow by name with synthetic fixtures
  - show step logs and final status deterministically

- `wa audit`
  - show recent actions
  - filters and redaction behavior

- `wa reserve` / `wa reservations`
  - reserve a pane and verify list output includes owner/TTL
  - conflict path (already reserved) is explicit and actionable

- `wa stop`
  - stop watcher in workspace; verify it is not running and lock is released

## Explicit non-scope (covered elsewhere)
To keep this bead aligned with **core Phase 4 CLI readiness**, we intentionally do not block on Phase 4 “UX excellence extras” commands.

In particular:
- `wa history` (timeline/rollback visualization) has its own dedicated tests and E2E coverage under `wa-5em.9`.

## Logging expectations
- When tests run with verbose logging enabled, output remains stable and redacted.
- Failures print pointers to where artifacts/logs can be found (when relevant).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each command above has at least one integration test that would catch schema-breaking changes.
- Exit codes and error codes are stable across releases.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - For every command tested in plain mode, assert there are no ANSI escapes.
  - Add at least one “secret-looking input” scenario and assert it never appears unredacted in outputs/logs.

- Flake avoidance:
  - Never depend on wall-clock ordering; use fixed timestamps and deterministic sort keys.
